name: Eric Dubay
description: Eric Dubay is an American conspiracy theorist best known for his ardent promotion of the flat Earth theory. He argues that the Earth is not a globe but a flat, stationary disc, covered by a dome. His theories are primarily disseminated through his website, YouTube channel, and books. Dubay's flat Earth model often incorporates elements of other conspiracy theories, such as the belief that NASA and other space agencies are involved in a global conspiracy to conceal the "truth" about the Earth's shape. He posits that the globe Earth is a deliberate deception designed to control and manipulate the population. Dubay's arguments typically rely on selective interpretations of scientific principles, anecdotal evidence, and personal observations. He often cites the lack of perceived curvature in large bodies of water and the apparent behavior of celestial objects as evidence for his claims. Mainstream science overwhelmingly rejects the flat Earth theory, citing extensive evidence from astronomy, physics, and geodesy that confirms the Earth's spherical shape. This evidence includes satellite imagery, circumnavigation, observations of lunar eclipses, variations in gravity at different locations, and the Coriolis effect. Dubay's methods and claims have been widely criticized by scientists and skeptics for lacking empirical support, employing logical fallacies, and ignoring established scientific knowledge. His work is often viewed as pseudoscience and a distortion of scientific understanding.
subtitle: Flat Earth proponent and conspiracy theorist

claims.claim: The Earth is flat, not a globe.
claims.evidence: Lack of perceived curvature, alleged inconsistencies in NASA imagery, observations of celestial objects from a flat Earth perspective.
claims.criticism: Overwhelming scientific evidence from multiple fields (astronomy, physics, geodesy) confirms the Earth's spherical shape. Lack of curvature is due to the Earth's large size relative to human perception. NASA imagery is verifiable and consistent with a globe Earth.

claims.claim: NASA and other space agencies are part of a global conspiracy to conceal the flat Earth.
claims.evidence: Alleged anomalies in NASA footage, claims of manipulated images, and distrust of government institutions.
claims.criticism: The scale of such a conspiracy involving thousands of individuals across multiple organizations and nations is implausible. Open-source data and independent verification of space missions contradict this claim.

claims.claim: Gravity is not a force but rather the result of the Earth constantly accelerating upwards.
claims.evidence: Dubay's interpretation of physics and selective use of scientific concepts.
claims.criticism: This contradicts established physics and the well-tested theory of general relativity, which explains gravity as the curvature of spacetime caused by mass and energy. Acceleration would require an impossible amount of energy.

keyFigures.name: Eric Dubay
keyFigures.role: Proponent, author, and promoter of the flat Earth theory

keyFigures.name: Neil deGrasse Tyson
keyFigures.role: Astrophysicist and outspoken critic of the flat Earth theory

mythologyConnections: Ancient cosmologies that posited a flat Earth (e.g., some interpretations of Norse mythology)
Reinterpretation of religious texts to support a flat Earth

timeline.event: Dubay begins promoting the flat Earth theory online.
Dubay publishes his book, 'The Flat Earth Conspiracy'.
Dubay continues to disseminate his theories through his website and YouTube channel.

sources.author: Eric Dubay
sources.work: 200 Proofs Earth is Not a Spinning Ball
sources.title: Eric Dubay

variants: 200 Proofs Earth is Not a Spinning Ball

worshipPractices.name: The Unveiling Ritual
worshipPractices.description: Adherents gather to symbolically 'unveil' the truth, often involving the sharing of Dubay's videos and literature. This ritualistic sharing aims to awaken others to the perceived deception of the 'globe earth' narrative. Emphasis is placed on independent research and questioning established scientific consensus.

worshipPractices.name: Horizon Gazing
worshipPractices.description: Followers engage in prolonged observation of the horizon, seeking visual 'proof' of the flat earth. They believe that the lack of visible curvature over long distances validates their perspective. This practice is often accompanied by measurements and photographic documentation intended to debunk spherical earth claims.

festivals.name: Flat Earth Convention
festivals.description: These conventions feature presentations, workshops, and social events centered around flat Earth beliefs. Attendees share evidence, discuss theories, and network with like-minded individuals. The conventions serve as a communal reinforcement of their shared worldview and a platform for disseminating information.

festivals.name: The Equinox Experiment
festivals.description: Followers conduct independent experiments during the equinoxes, aiming to disprove the heliocentric model. Common experiments involve observations of shadow lengths and positions, which they believe provide evidence for a flat, sun-centric system rather than a heliocentric one.

cultural.modernLegacy: Eric Dubay's influence manifests in the persistent popularity of flat Earth theories online and in fringe communities. His arguments and videos continue to be circulated, contributing to a broader trend of distrust in scientific institutions and the spread of misinformation. His work has inspired numerous other flat-earth content creators and fueled online debates about the shape of the Earth.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Loki is a trickster god known for spreading misinformation and challenging the established order of the Aesir, often leading to chaos and discord, mirroring Dubay's role in spreading conspiracy theories and challenging mainstream science.

Epimetheus, brother of Prometheus, is known for his lack of foresight and accepting Pandora's box, unleashing evils upon the world. This parallels Dubay's uncritical acceptance and dissemination of unsubstantiated claims, leading to the spread of misinformation.

Maya is the illusion or veil that obscures the true nature of reality in Hindu philosophy. Dubay's flat Earth theory can be seen as a modern manifestation of Maya, presenting a false reality that obscures the scientific understanding of the Earth's shape.

Apep is the serpent of chaos and darkness who constantly tries to devour the sun god Ra, disrupting the order of the cosmos. This mirrors Dubay's efforts to undermine established scientific understanding, creating chaos and doubt in the face of accepted knowledge.

associations.name: Chaos
associations.significance: His theories disrupt established scientific understanding, creating intellectual and social discord.

associations.name: Earth
associations.significance: Ironically, though his claims are unfounded, he fixates intensely on a distorted version of the Earth.

associations.name: Serpent
associations.significance: Like the serpent of temptation, he offers a tempting alternative to established knowledge, leading people astray.

associations.name: Illusion
associations.significance: His flat Earth theory is fundamentally based on the creation and propagation of illusion.

associations.name: Lunar Eclipse
associations.significance: His ideas obscure the light of scientific understanding, creating a shadow of misinformation.

associations.name: One
associations.significance: He presents a singular, simplified view of the world, rejecting complexity and nuance.

associations.name: Grey
associations.significance: His theories exist in a grey area between skepticism and outright denial of established facts.

companions.name: Atlas
companions.description: In Greek Mythology, Atlas held up the sky. Eric Dubay would likely see Atlas as an ally in opposing the 'spherical' world view, albeit with a different model.

companions.name: Typhon
companions.description: Typhon, a monstrous giant in Greek mythology who challenged Zeus, could be seen as an ally in challenging the established order and mainstream scientific consensus.

companions.name: The Serpent of Midgard (Jormungandr)
companions.description: Jormungandr, the world serpent from Norse mythology encircling Midgard, could be symbolically associated with a flat Earth model with a physical boundary.

companions.name: The Firmament
companions.description: The firmament, a solid dome believed by some ancient cultures to cover the Earth, aligns with Dubay's flat Earth model and serves as a key component of his belief system.

companions.name: Maui
companions.description: In Polynesian mythology, Maui slowed the sun and lifted the sky, actions that could be interpreted by Dubay as altering celestial mechanics and spatial dimensions to fit his theory.

extendedContent.title: The Genesis of Flat Earth Advocacy
extendedContent.content: Eric Dubay's journey into flat Earth advocacy appears to have begun in the early 2000s, although specific details regarding his initial influences remain largely anecdotal, often relying on his own self-reported narratives. He gained prominence through his website, 'Atlantean Conspiracy,' and his prolific output of YouTube videos and self-published books. Unlike earlier proponents of flat Earth theory, such as Samuel Birley Rowbotham, who based their arguments primarily on religious interpretations and flawed scientific experiments, Dubay's approach is more explicitly aligned with modern conspiracy theories. He utilizes the internet to disseminate his views, exploiting the algorithms of social media platforms to reach a wider audience. His version of the flat Earth model often incorporates elements of other conspiracy theories, such as the belief that NASA is a fraudulent organization dedicated to concealing the 'truth' about the Earth's shape. He frequently cites supposed inconsistencies in scientific observations and photographs as evidence against the globe model, often relying on misrepresented or misinterpreted data to support his claims. Dubay's success lies not in original scholarship, but in repackaging existing flat Earth arguments with a contemporary conspiratorial framing.

extendedContent.title: Core Tenets of Dubay's Flat Earth Model
extendedContent.content: Dubay's flat Earth model is a variation on established flat Earth theories, positing that the Earth is a stationary disc with the Arctic at its center. The continents are arranged around the Arctic, and Antarctica forms an ice wall encircling the Earth, preventing anyone from falling off. The sun and moon are much smaller than commonly believed and orbit above the Earth, creating day and night. He claims that the stars are also located within the dome, and their movement is explained by a complex system of gears and mechanisms. One central argument relies on the perceived lack of curvature. Dubay and his followers often perform experiments, such as measuring the level of water over long distances, to demonstrate the absence of curvature. These experiments, however, often fail to account for atmospheric refraction and other factors that affect measurements. He further argues that gravity is not a natural force, but rather a result of the Earth constantly accelerating upwards. This upward acceleration, he claims, creates the illusion of gravity. Dubay often dismisses scientific evidence, such as satellite imagery and GPS technology, as elaborate hoaxes perpetrated by governments and scientific institutions to maintain control over the population.

extendedContent.title: The Appeal and Spread of Dubay's Ideas
extendedContent.content: The appeal of Dubay's ideas, like many conspiracy theories, stems from a combination of factors, including distrust of authority, a desire for simple explanations to complex phenomena, and a sense of belonging to a community of like-minded individuals. His accessible and engaging presentation style, coupled with the widespread availability of his content on platforms like YouTube, has allowed him to reach a global audience. The internet has facilitated the creation of a vibrant online flat Earth community, where followers can share information, conduct 'experiments,' and support one another's beliefs. Dubay's work resonates particularly with individuals who feel alienated from mainstream society or who are skeptical of scientific consensus. He presents himself as a truth-seeker battling against a powerful establishment, a narrative that appeals to those who feel disenfranchised. Furthermore, the simplicity of the flat Earth model offers a comforting alternative to the complexities of modern science, providing a sense of control and understanding in an increasingly uncertain world. The community aspect is crucial, as it reinforces belief through shared experiences and validation within the group.

extendedContent.title: Criticism and Debunking of Dubay's Claims
extendedContent.content: Dubay's flat Earth claims have been widely debunked by scientists and educators, who point to a vast body of evidence supporting the spherical Earth model. Numerous experiments, observations, and technological advancements, such as satellite imagery, GPS technology, and air travel, rely on the Earth being a globe. The curvature of the Earth can be observed in various ways, such as by watching ships disappear hull first over the horizon or by observing different constellations from different latitudes. Furthermore, the existence of time zones, the behavior of the Coriolis effect, and the phenomenon of lunar eclipses all provide strong evidence for a spherical Earth. Critics also point to the internal inconsistencies and logical fallacies within Dubay's arguments. His reliance on selectively chosen data, misrepresentation of scientific concepts, and unsubstantiated claims undermines the credibility of his theories. While Dubay often dismisses scientific evidence as a conspiracy, he fails to provide any credible evidence to support his own claims. His arguments often rely on anecdotal evidence, personal experiences, and misinterpretations of scientific data, rather than rigorous scientific methodology.
